Home → Demo Hub → Validator Map

Validator wall

The system is proven by independent validators, not by claims.

There are 280 validator scripts in the repo. They work as a flat E2E wall, with package-specific checks for the external demo, stakeholder presentation, and premium demo hub.

What validators prove

They prove file presence, marker presence, latest-status consistency, and safety boundaries. They do not enable runtime behavior.

Dynamic latest-status validator

Confirms the live dashboard still reports MVP-50 as the latest verified milestone.

Master validator wall

Blocks branch changes outside the allowed surfaces and keeps the release story coherent.

External demo validator

Checks the external demo package and its safety markers.

Stakeholder presentation validator

Checks the stakeholder package files and presentation metadata.

Premium demo validator

Checks the browser-viewable microsite, its report, and its safety labels.

No nested E2E chains

Each validator is independent so failures stay local and readable.

Flat E2E strategy

What the wall is doing

What it proves

The wall proves the demo package still says what the repo says and that the safety markers are still present.

What it does not prove

It does not run commands, trigger automation, send alerts, or mutate live systems.

Why the flat pattern matters

It keeps validation modular and prevents nested test chains from masking failures.

Future review needs

Runtime activation would still need a separate security review and operational approval.

Agent Command Center, the demo pages, registry presentation, dashboard copy, visual layout, simulator materials, validator descriptions, and stakeholder presentation materials are protected project materials. No license, sale, transfer, or permission to reuse is granted by viewing this demo.

This site is a static, read-only demonstration. It does not enable live automation, command execution, database writes, Supabase mutations, deployment actions, rollback execution, alert sending, or runtime activation.

Third-party names, platforms, and services referenced for context remain the property of their respective owners.
